name: chargebee
description: Read Chargebee subscriptions, customers, and invoices and create customers for subscription billing

envVars.name: CHARGEBEE_API_KEY
envVars.description: Chargebee API key, used as the HTTP Basic username (Settings → Configure Chargebee → API Keys and Webhooks)

envVars.name: CHARGEBEE_API_PASSWORD
envVars.description: HTTP Basic password — Chargebee expects this to stay empty; leave it as an empty string

envVars.name: CHARGEBEE_SITE
envVars.description: Chargebee site name (the subdomain of your Chargebee account, e.g. acme or acme-test)

tools.name: List Subscriptions
tools.description: List subscriptions with optional status or customer filtering

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

status[is].description: Filter by subscription status: future, in_trial, active, non_renewing, paused, cancelled, or transferred

customer_id[is].description: Only return subscriptions belonging to this customer ID

offset.description: Pagination cursor: pass the next_offset value from the previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: nested customer and card objects returned alongside each subscription

tools.name: Get Subscription
tools.description: Retrieve a single subscription with its plan, status, and billing details

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

tools.name: List Customers
tools.description: List customers with optional email filtering

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

email[is].description: Only return customers with this exact email address

offset.description: Pagination cursor: pass the next_offset value from the previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: nested card objects returned alongside each customer

tools.name: List Invoices
tools.description: List invoices with optional status, customer, or subscription filtering

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

status[is].description: Filter by invoice status: paid, posted, payment_due, not_paid, voided, or pending

customer_id[is].description: Only return invoices for this customer ID

subscription_id[is].description: Only return invoices for this subscription ID

offset.description: Pagination cursor: pass the next_offset value from the previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: invoice line items, discounts, and linked payment details

tools.name: List Item Prices
tools.description: List item prices (plans, addons, charges) in the catalog to find IDs and pricing for subscriptions

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

item_id[is].description: Only return prices belonging to this item ID

item_type[is].description: Filter by item type: plan, addon, or charge

status[is].description: Filter by status: active, archived, or deleted

currency_code[is].description: Filter by ISO 4217 currency code, e.g. USD

limit.description: Number of item prices to return (1-100)

offset.description: Pagination cursor: pass the next_offset value from the previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: tier, tax, and accounting detail fields on each item price

tools.name: Get Invoice
tools.description: Retrieve a single invoice with its line items, amounts due, and linked payments

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

tools.name: List Transactions
tools.description: List payment-level transactions (payments, refunds, authorizations) behind invoices, e.g. to review failed payments

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

customer_id[is].description: Only return transactions for this customer ID

subscription_id[is].description: Only return transactions for this subscription ID

type[is].description: Filter by transaction type: authorization, payment, refund, or payment_reversal

status[is].description: Filter by status: in_progress, success, voided, failure, timeout, needs_attention, late_failure, or fraud_flag

offset.description: Pagination cursor: pass the next_offset value from the previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: linked invoice, payment, and refund detail objects on each transaction

tools.name: Create Customer
tools.description: Create a new customer record in Chargebee

site.description: Chargebee site name (subdomain), e.g. acme or acme-test — same value as CHARGEBEE_SITE

id.description: Customer ID (max 50 chars); auto-generated by Chargebee if omitted

prompts.title: Active subscriptions
prompts.prompt: List my active Chargebee subscriptions and summarize them by plan.

prompts.title: Unpaid invoices
prompts.prompt: Show Chargebee invoices with status payment_due or not_paid and which customers they belong to.

prompts.title: Customer lookup
prompts.prompt: Look up a Chargebee customer by email and show their subscriptions and recent invoices.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Chargebee API Setup

steps.title: Create a Chargebee account
steps.description: Sign up at https://www.chargebee.com. A test site (e.g. acme-test.chargebee.com) is created automatically when you sign up — use it for development before switching to your live site.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: In the Chargebee app go to Settings → Configure Chargebee → API Keys and Webhooks → API Keys, click Add an API Key, choose Full-Access Key, and copy the generated key.

steps.title: Set environment variables
steps.description: Add CHARGEBEE_API_KEY (the key from step 2) and CHARGEBEE_SITE (your site subdomain, e.g. acme-test) to your .env. Leave CHARGEBEE_API_PASSWORD unset or empty — Chargebee uses the API key as the HTTP Basic username with an empty password.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Subscriptions with site set to your CHARGEBEE_SITE value. All requests go to https://{site}.chargebee.com/api/v2 over HTTPS.

notes: API keys are environment-specific: your test site and live site have separate keys and separate site subdomains.
Chargebee filters use bracket operators in query parameter names, e.g. status[is]=active or email[is]=jane@example.com.
List endpoints paginate with an opaque next_offset string — pass it back as the offset parameter to fetch the next page.
